soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The sharpness polytope is defined over binary response types for Y(a,m), but the locked setup has Y in [0,1] and asks the finite table to reproduce the full observable law P, so the latent projection is not well-posed unless Y is made binary or the polytope is redefined for bounded mean-response kernels.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption total-effect already states that the total-effect functional has no residual loading on the shared coordinate, making beta_D+beta_I=0 essentially assumed; either weaken the assumption or relabel the result as an algebraic theorem/sanity check.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Resolve the Y support mismatch by choosing one convention: binary Y with a genuine finite response-type polytope, or Y in [0,1] with a finite-dimensional mean-kernel/linear-programming projection that does not claim to reproduce the full outcome distribution.
Turn Conjecture 2 into a theorem conditional on total-effect zero-loading, or weaken Assumption total-effect so the zero-loading conclusion is not assumed in advance.

Add an explicit two-stratum binary example where the marginal PNDE and TNIE intervals are both nondegenerate but the coupled set is a strict line segment; this would make the field-tier contribution more legible.

Add a direct comparison to generic symbolic-bounds/Autobounds or Balke-Pearl finite-LP machinery, explaining why the new content is the shared proxy-null mediation geometry rather than generic projection sharpness.

recommended_next_step: Patch the outcome/latent-polytope convention and de-tautologize the total-effect result; the novelty kernel can then proceed to Stage 0.
